name: Whoopie Pies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
2. Lightly grease two cookie sheets; set aside.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, cream shortening and sugar until light and fluffy. Add egg and vanilla; beat.
5. Add dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk, starting and ending with flour. Mix well.
6. Drop batter by tablespoonfuls (make BIG cookies) onto greased cookie sheets. Try to make the mounds as round as possible; they'll look better when done. Whatever shape you drop the dough, that's the shape the cookie will be.
7. Space cookies at least 2 inches apart. They spread out quite a bit. Make sure you make an even number of cookies—two halves make one Whoopie Pie.
8. Bake 8-10 minutes or until cookies spring back when lightly touched and are no longer shiny on top.
9. Leave cookies on sheets for a few minutes to cool completely before filling.
1. Cream butter and shortening until light and fluffy.
2. Beat in marshmallow creme. Add vanilla; beat well.
3. Add confectioners' sugar; beat until smooth.
4. Spread 1 tbsp of filling onto one half of the cookies. Top with the other half. If cookies are bigger, add more filling.
5. These freeze well but must be airtight to avoid sogginess. Carl likes them frozen! For ice cream sandwiches, place a slice of ice cream between the cookies instead of filling. Original recipes called for more shortening in the filling, making it too greasy. I prefer less; a little shortening smooths the filling better.
